sources.notes: Count how many cape orientations can produce the observed visible-wizard counts.
Official editorial shows adjacent visible counts determine all orientations up to the first wizard's choice.

annotation.summary: Given the number of visible wizards from each position, count the possible left/right cape orientations that match it.
annotation.constraints: n is large, so all possible orientations cannot be enumerated, but the structure leaves only two candidates.
annotation.core_idea: Moving from i to i+1 only changes the visibility of wizards i and i+1, so adjacent visible counts can differ by at most 1. Moreover, whether a_i and a_{i+1} are equal or differ determines whether the two adjacent capes have opposite or equal directions. Thus fixing the first wizard's direction determines the entire orientation; try both possibilities and simulate the visible counts to validate.

solution_variants.name: two-orientations-check
solution_variants.summary: Construct the two possible orientation arrays determined by the first cape and verify each against the observed counts.

tags.evidence: After the structural observation, only two global orientation candidates remain and both are checked directly.
The main work is correctly constructing and validating the visibility simulation for the two candidates.
Adjacent count differences constrain whether neighboring capes must be equal or opposite.
